activities_projects.title: Case Study of Political Party Functions
activities_projects.objective: Analyse how political parties perform democratic functions in real life
steps: Select a recent political event reported in the news
Identify which political party is involved
Explain which functions of political parties are visible in the event
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students connect textbook theory with real political events
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of analysis and relevance of example (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Comparative Study of Party Systems
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate the effectiveness of different party systems
steps: Research one country with a two-party system and one with a multiparty system
Compare political stability and representation in both systems
Write a conclusion about which system works better and why
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students critically evaluate political systems globally

activities_projects.title: Ideology Analysis of Political Parties
activities_projects.objective: Understand ideological differences among major parties
steps: Select two national political parties in India
Study their ideology, goals and major policies
Compare their approaches to national development
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students analyse ideological differences in politics
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of comparison and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Role of Opposition Study
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate how opposition parties strengthen democracy
steps: Find an example where opposition parties criticised government policies
Explain the issue and the opposition’s arguments
Evaluate whether the criticism improved democratic accountability
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the democratic importance of opposition

activities_projects.title: Political Party Reform Proposal
activities_projects.objective: Design reforms to improve the functioning of political parties
steps: List the main challenges faced by political parties
Analyse why these problems exist
Propose at least three reforms and explain their benefits
activities_projects.evaluation: Practicality and clarity of proposed reforms (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Election Campaign Strategy Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Understand campaign strategies used by political parties
steps: Observe recent election campaigns of a political party
Identify methods used to attract voters
Evaluate which strategies are most effective

activities_projects.title: Regional Party Influence Study
activities_projects.objective: Examine how state parties influence national politics
steps: Select a regional party in India
Study its role in coalition governments
Explain how regional interests influence national decision-making
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the role of regional diversity in politics

activities_projects.title: Public Opinion Survey on Political Parties
activities_projects.objective: Study citizens’ attitudes toward political parties
steps: Survey at least 10 people about their trust in political parties
Record reasons for their opinions
Analyse the results and identify common trends
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn research and data analysis skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of survey and interpretation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Political Party Manifesto Study
activities_projects.objective: Analyse promises made by political parties during elections
steps: Collect the election manifesto of a political party
List major promises made to voters
Evaluate which promises address major social issues
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand policy commitments in elections

activities_projects.title: Design Your Own Political Party
steps: Create a fictional political party
Write its ideology, symbol and key policies
Explain how your party will avoid the challenges faced by real parties
